positions.claim: AI-driven labor displacement will cause a structural economic crisis that traditional monetary policy cannot address, requiring direct fiscal transfers to households
implicitAssumptions: The velocity of AI capability improvement exceeds the economy's ability to redeploy displaced workers into new roles
Political institutions will respond reactively to economic crisis rather than proactively implementing preventive measures
Labor's declining share of GDP cannot be offset by productivity gains distributed through existing market mechanisms

positions.claim: White-collar job displacement represents a fundamentally different economic phenomenon than historical technological disruption because AI targets the cognitive labor that comprises 50% of US employment
implicitAssumptions: Historical patterns of technological job displacement depended on cognitive work remaining a human monopoly
The economic value of human intelligence was artificially inflated by its scarcity rather than its intrinsic worth
Institutions built on assumptions of scarce human intelligence (mortgage underwriting, tax systems) cannot adapt quickly enough to abundance

positions.claim: Companies will aggressively cut headcount and reinvest savings into AI capabilities, creating a self-reinforcing cycle of margin expansion for compute owners and unemployment for workers
implicitAssumptions: Corporate incentive structures prioritize margin expansion over employment stability even when aggregate demand effects are negative
AI capability improvements will continue to reduce costs faster than they create new labor demand
Equity markets will reward labor cost reduction before pricing in the demand-side consequences of unemployment

positions.claim: Being bullish on AI capabilities necessarily implies bearish outcomes for labor markets and traditional economic structures, creating a paradox where technological optimism leads to market pessimism
implicitAssumptions: AI capability gains translate more directly to labor substitution than to new market creation
The time lag between job displacement and new job creation is long enough to cause systemic economic stress
Markets will eventually price in unemployment effects despite initially rewarding margin expansion

positions.claim: Software companies face existential repricing risk from agentic AI even before actual business impact materializes, as the threat alone justifies valuation compression
implicitAssumptions: Agentic AI will commoditize software functionality faster than software companies can capture AI-driven productivity gains
The 'ARR as recurring' assumption breaks down when AI agents can switch between tools frictionlessly
Software company moats depend on switching costs and integration complexity that AI agents eliminate

positions.claim: NVIDIA's dominance in AI training is overstated because Google's vertical integration with TPUs demonstrates that frontier models can be trained without NVIDIA hardware
implicitAssumptions: Training efficiency gains from vertical integration (co-designing chips and models) outweigh NVIDIA's ecosystem advantages
Other hyperscalers will follow Google's path toward custom silicon rather than remaining NVIDIA-dependent
The marginal cost advantage of TPUs is large enough to justify the fixed costs of chip design and fabrication

positions.claim: AI infrastructure expansion will drive natural gas prices structurally higher as datacenter power demand tightens supply-demand balances
implicitAssumptions: AI training and inference compute scales faster than renewable energy capacity can be deployed
Natural gas remains the marginal power source for datacenter expansion despite renewable energy goals
Datacenter power demand is relatively price-inelastic compared to other natural gas demand sources

positions.claim: Private credit exposure to software companies represents systemic risk as ARR-backed loans default when AI makes recurring revenue non-recurring
implicitAssumptions: Private credit underwriting models assume software revenue persistence that AI disruption invalidates
Household savings exposure through annuities creates contagion pathways from software defaults to broader financial system
The private credit market's growth to $2.5 trillion concentrates rather than diversifies software company risk

positions.claim: Asymmetric warfare enabled by cheap drones creates sustained defense spending acceleration that favors smaller specialized companies over traditional defense primes
implicitAssumptions: Military procurement cycles will adapt quickly enough to favor drone specialists over traditional contractors
The cost-effectiveness of drones (cheap assets destroying expensive infrastructure) represents a permanent shift rather than a temporary asymmetry
Geopolitical fracturing accelerates rather than consolidates, sustaining defense spending growth

positions.claim: Understanding semiconductor supply chain dynamics 6-12 months forward provides asymmetric trading opportunities that surface-level 'GPU-deep' AI analysis misses
implicitAssumptions: Supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints are more predictive of AI stock performance than model capability announcements
Post-Traumatic Supply Disorder (overcorrection from past shortages) creates persistent supply-demand imbalances
Most market participants lack the technical depth to translate semiconductor supply dynamics into investment theses

positions.claim: Traditional payment networks face moat erosion as stablecoins on Solana or Ethereum L2s enable near-zero-cost settlement, targeting the 2-3% interchange rate for elimination
implicitAssumptions: Regulatory frameworks will permit stablecoin payment systems to compete directly with card networks
Consumer and merchant adoption of crypto-based payments will overcome UX and trust barriers
The value of payment network effects (acceptance, fraud protection) is smaller than the cost savings from eliminating interchange fees

positions.claim: Liquidity injections and rate cuts beginning in 2024 will create bullish conditions for risk assets despite underlying economic deterioration from AI displacement
implicitAssumptions: Monetary policy operates on shorter time horizons than structural labor market changes, creating temporary asset price support
Political pressure from midterm elections forces liquidity provision regardless of long-term economic fundamentals
Equity markets will respond to liquidity conditions before pricing in AI-driven unemployment effects
